NKJV.1: A Psalm of David. The LORD is my light and my salvation; Whom shall I fear? The LORD is the strength of my life; Of whom shall I be afraid?
NKJV.2: When the wicked came against me To eat up my flesh, My enemies and foes, They stumbled and fell.
NKJV.3: Though an army may encamp against me, My heart shall not fear; Though war may rise against me, In this I will be confident.
NKJV.4: One thing I have desired of the LORD, That will I seek: That I may dwell in the house of the LORD All the days of my life, To behold the beauty of the LORD, And to inquire in His temple.
NKJV.5: For in the time of trouble He shall hide me in His pavilion; In the secret place of His tabernacle He shall hide me; He shall set me high upon a rock.
NKJV.6: And now my head shall be lifted up above my enemies all around me; Therefore I will offer sacrifices of joy in His tabernacle; I will sing, yes, I will sing praises to the LORD.
NKJV.7: Hear, O LORD, when I cry with my voice! Have mercy also upon me, and answer me.
NKJV.8: When You said, “Seek My face,” My heart said to You, “Your face, LORD, I will seek.”
NKJV.9: Do not hide Your face from me; Do not turn Your servant away in anger; You have been my help; Do not leave me nor forsake me, O God of my salvation.
NKJV.10: When my father and my mother forsake me, Then the LORD will take care of me.
NKJV.11: Teach me Your way, O LORD, And lead me in a smooth path, because of my enemies.
NKJV.12: Do not deliver me to the will of my adversaries; For false witnesses have risen against me, And such as breathe out violence.
NKJV.13: I would have lost heart, unless I had believed That I would see the goodness of the LORD In the land of the living.
NKJV.14: Wait on the LORD; Be of good courage, And He shall strengthen your heart; Wait, I say, on the LORD!

KJV.9: Hide<S>5641</S> not thy face<S>6440</S> far from me; put<S>5186</S> not thy servant<S>5650</S> away<S>5186</S> in anger<S>639</S>: thou hast been my help<S>5833</S>; leave<S>5203</S> me not, neither forsake<S>5800</S> me, O God<S>430</S> of my salvation<S>3468</S>.
KJV.10: When my father<S>1</S> and my mother<S>517</S> forsake<S>5800</S> me, then the LORD<S>3068</S> will take me up<S>622</S>. <sup>take: Heb. gather me</sup>
KJV.11: Teach<S>3384</S> me thy way<S>1870</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S>, and lead<S>5148</S> me in a plain<S>4334</S> path<S>734</S>, because of mine enemies<S>8324</S>. <sup>a plain: Heb. a way of plainness</sup> <sup>mine: Heb. those which observe me</sup>

ESV.1: The LORD is my light and my salvation; whom shall I fear? The LORD is the stronghold  of my life; of whom shall I be afraid?
ESV.2: When evildoers assail me to eat up my flesh, my adversaries and foes, it is they who stumble and fall.
ESV.3: Though an army encamp against me, my heart shall not fear; though war arise against me, yet  I will be confident.
ESV.4: One thing have I asked of the LORD, that will I seek after: that I may dwell in the house of the LORD all the days of my life, to gaze upon the beauty of the LORD and to inquire  in his temple.
ESV.5: For he will hide me in his shelter in the day of trouble; he will conceal me under the cover of his tent; he will lift me high upon a rock.
ESV.6: And now my head shall be lifted up above my enemies all around me, and I will offer in his tent sacrifices with shouts of joy; I will sing and make melody to the LORD.
ESV.7: Hear, O LORD, when I cry aloud; be gracious to me and answer me!
ESV.8: You have said, “Seek  my face.” My heart says to you, “Your face, LORD, do I seek.”
ESV.9: Hide not your face from me. Turn not your servant away in anger, O you who have been my help. Cast me not off; forsake me not, O God of my salvation!
ESV.10: For my father and my mother have forsaken me, but the LORD will take me in.
ESV.11: Teach me your way, O LORD, and lead me on a level path because of my enemies.
ESV.12: Give me not up to the will of my adversaries; for false witnesses have risen against me, and they breathe out violence.
ESV.13: I believe that I shall look  upon the goodness of the LORD in the land of the living!
ESV.14: Wait for the LORD; be strong, and let your heart take courage; wait for the LORD!

NASB.1: [A Psalm] of David. The LORD is my light and my salvation; Whom shall I fear? The LORD is the defense of my life; Whom shall I dread?
NASB.2: When evildoers came upon me to devour my flesh, My adversaries and my enemies, they stumbled and fell.
NASB.3: Though a host encamp against me, My heart will not fear; Though war arise against me, In [spite of] this I shall be confident.
NASB.4: One thing I have asked from the LORD, that I shall seek: That I may dwell in the house of the LORD all the days of my life, To behold the beauty of the LORD And to meditate in His temple.
NASB.5: For in the day of trouble He will conceal me in His tabernacle; In the secret place of His tent He will hide me; He will lift me up on a rock.
NASB.6: And now my head will be lifted up above my enemies around me, And I will offer in His tent sacrifices with shouts of joy; I will sing, yes, I will sing praises to the LORD.
NASB.7: Hear, O LORD, when I cry with my voice, And be gracious to me and answer me.
NASB.8: [When You said], 'Seek My face,' my heart said to You, 'Your face, O LORD, I shall seek.'
NASB.9: Do not hide Your face from me, Do not turn Your servant away in anger; You have been my help; Do not abandon me nor forsake me, O God of my salvation!
NASB.10: For my father and my mother have forsaken me, But the LORD will take me up.
NASB.11: Teach me Your way, O LORD, And lead me in a level path Because of my foes.
NASB.12: Do not deliver me over to the desire of my adversaries, For false witnesses have risen against me, And such as breathe out violence.
NASB.13: [I would have despaired] unless I had believed that I would see the goodness of the LORD In the land of the living.
NASB.14: Wait for the LORD; Be strong and let your heart take courage; Yes, wait for the LORD.

NLT.1:
The LORD is my light and my salvation —
so why should I be afraid?
The LORD is my fortress, protecting me from danger,
so why should I tremble?
NLT.2:
When evil people come to devour me,
when my enemies and foes attack me,
they will stumble and fall.
NLT.3:
Though a mighty army surrounds me,
my heart will not be afraid.
Even if I am attacked,
I will remain confident.
NLT.4:
The one thing I ask of the LORD —
the thing I seek most —
is to live in the house of the LORD all the days of my life,
delighting in the LORD’s perfections
and meditating in his Temple.
NLT.5:
For he will conceal me there when troubles come;
he will hide me in his sanctuary.
He will place me out of reach on a high rock.
NLT.6:
Then I will hold my head high
above my enemies who surround me.
At his sanctuary I will offer sacrifices with shouts of joy,
singing and praising the LORD with music.
NLT.7:
Hear me as I pray, O LORD.
Be merciful and answer me!
NLT.8:
My heart has heard you say, “Come and talk with me.”
And my heart responds, “LORD, I am coming.”
NLT.9:
Do not turn your back on me.
Do not reject your servant in anger.
You have always been my helper.
Don’t leave me now; don’t abandon me,
O God of my salvation!
NLT.10:
Even if my father and mother abandon me,
the LORD will hold me close.
NLT.11:
Teach me how to live, O LORD.
Lead me along the right path,
for my enemies are waiting for me.
NLT.12:
Do not let me fall into their hands.
For they accuse me of things I’ve never done;
with every breath they threaten me with violence.
NLT.13:
Yet I am confident I will see the LORD’s goodness
while I am here in the land of the living.
NLT.14:
Wait patiently for the LORD.
Be brave and courageous.
Yes, wait patiently for the LORD.

WEB.1: By David. Yahweh is my light and my salvation. Whom shall I fear? Yahweh is the strength of my life. Of whom shall I be afraid?
WEB.2: When evildoers came at me to eat up my flesh, even my adversaries and my foes, they stumbled and fell.
WEB.3: Though an army should encamp against me, my heart shall not fear. Though war should rise against me, even then I will be confident.
WEB.4: One thing I have asked of Yahweh, that I will seek after: that I may dwell in Yahweh’s house all the days of my life, to see Yahweh’s beauty, and to inquire in his temple.
WEB.5: For in the day of trouble he will keep me secretly in his pavilion. In the covert of his tabernacle he will hide me. He will lift me up on a rock.
WEB.6: Now my head will be lifted up above my enemies around me; I will offer sacrifices of joy in his tent. I will sing, yes, I will sing praises to Yahweh.
WEB.7: Hear, Yahweh, when I cry with my voice. Have mercy also on me, and answer me.
WEB.8: When you said, “Seek my face,” my heart said to you, “I will seek your face, Yahweh.”
WEB.9: Don’t hide your face from me. Don’t put your servant away in anger. You have been my help. Don’t abandon me, neither forsake me, God of my salvation.
WEB.10: When my father and my mother forsake me, then Yahweh will take me up.
WEB.11: Teach me your way, Yahweh. Lead me in a straight path, because of my enemies.
WEB.12: Don’t deliver me over to the desire of my adversaries, for false witnesses have risen up against me, such as breathe out cruelty.
WEB.13: I am still confident of this: I will see the goodness of Yahweh in the land of the living.
WEB.14: Wait for Yahweh. Be strong, and let your heart take courage. Yes, wait for Yahweh.

AMP.1:
A Psalm of David. The LORD is my light and my salvation—
Whom shall I fear?
The LORD is the refuge and fortress of my life—
Whom shall I dread?
AMP.2:
When the wicked came against me to eat up my flesh,
My adversaries and my enemies, they stumbled and fell.
AMP.3:
Though an army encamp against me,
My heart will not fear;
Though war arise against me,
Even in this I am confident.
AMP.4:
One thing I have asked of the LORD, and that I will seek:
That I may dwell in the house of the LORD [in His presence] all the days of my life,
To gaze upon the beauty [the delightful loveliness and majestic grandeur] of the LORD
And to meditate in His temple.
AMP.5:
For in the day of trouble He will hide me in His shelter;
In the secret place of His tent He will hide me;
He will lift me up on a rock.
AMP.6:
And now my head will be lifted up above my enemies around me,
In His tent I will offer sacrifices with shouts of joy;
I will sing, yes, I will sing praises to the LORD.
AMP.7:
Hear, O LORD, when I cry aloud;
Be gracious and compassionate to me and answer me.
AMP.8:
When You said, "Seek My face [in prayer, require My presence as your greatest need]," my heart said to You,
"Your face, O LORD, I will seek [on the authority of Your word]."
AMP.9:
Do not hide Your face from me,
Do not turn Your servant away in anger;
You have been my help;
Do not abandon me nor leave me,
O God of my salvation!
AMP.10:
Although my father and my mother have abandoned me,
Yet the LORD will take me up [adopt me as His child].
AMP.11:
Teach me Your way, O LORD,
And lead me on a level path
Because of my enemies [who lie in wait].
AMP.12:
Do not give me up to the will of my adversaries,
For false witnesses have come against me;
They breathe out violence.
AMP.13:
I would have despaired had I not believed that I would see the goodness of the LORD
In the land of the living.
AMP.14:
Wait for and confidently expect the LORD;
Be strong and let your heart take courage;
Yes, wait for and confidently expect the LORD.

MSG.1:
A David psalm
Light, space, zest—
that's God!
So, with him on my side I'm fearless,
afraid of no one and nothing.
MSG.2:
When vandal hordes ride down
ready to eat me alive,
Those bullies and toughs
fall flat on their faces.
MSG.3:
When besieged,
I'm calm as a baby.
When all hell breaks loose,
I'm collected and cool.
MSG.4:
I'm asking God for one thing,
only one thing:
To live with him in his house
my whole life long.
I'll contemplate his beauty;
I'll study at his feet.
MSG.5:
That's the only quiet, secure place
in a noisy world,
The perfect getaway,
far from the buzz of traffic.
MSG.6:
God holds me head and shoulders
above all who try to pull me down.
I'm headed for his place to offer anthems
that will raise the roof!
Already I'm singing God-songs;
I'm making music to God.
MSG.7:
Listen, God, I'm calling at the top of my lungs:
"Be good to me! Answer me!"
MSG.8:
When my heart whispered, "Seek God,"
my whole being replied,
"I'm seeking him!"
MSG.9:
Don't hide from me now!
You've always been right there for me;
don't turn your back on me now.
Don't throw me out, don't abandon me;
you've always kept the door open.
MSG.10:
My father and mother walked out and left me,
but God took me in.
MSG.11:
Point me down your highway, God;
direct me along a well-lighted street;
show my enemies whose side you're on.
MSG.12:
Don't throw me to the dogs,
those liars who are out to get me,
filling the air with their threats.
MSG.13:
I'm sure now I'll see God's goodness
in the exuberant earth.
MSG.14:
Stay with God!
Take heart. Don't quit.
I'll say it again:
Stay with God.

NIV.1:
Psalm 27
Of David.
The Lord is my light and my salvation—
whom shall I fear?
The Lord is the stronghold of my life—
of whom shall I be afraid?
NIV.2:
When evil men advance against me
to devour my flesh,
when my enemies and my foes attack me,
they will stumble and fall.
NIV.3:
Though an army besiege me,
my heart will not fear;
though war break out against me,
even then will I be confident.
NIV.4:
One thing I ask of the Lord,
this is what I seek:
that I may dwell in the house of the Lord
all the days of my life,
to gaze upon the beauty of the Lord
and to seek him in his temple.
NIV.5:
For in the day of trouble
he will keep me safe in his dwelling;
he will hide me in the shelter of his tabernacle
and set me high upon a rock.
NIV.6:
Then my head will be exalted
above the enemies who surround me;
at his tabernacle will I sacrifice with shouts of joy;
I will sing and make music to the Lord.
NIV.7:
Hear my voice when I call, O Lord;
be merciful to me and answer me.
NIV.8:
My heart says of you, “Seek his face!”
Your face, Lord, I will seek.
NIV.9:
Do not hide your face from me,
do not turn your servant away in anger;
you have been my helper.
Do not reject me or forsake me,
O God my Savior.
NIV.10:
Though my father and mother forsake me,
the Lord will receive me.
NIV.11:
Teach me your way, O Lord;
lead me in a straight path
because of my oppressors.
NIV.12:
Do not turn me over to the desire of my foes,
for false witnesses rise up against me,
breathing out violence.
NIV.13:
I am still confident of this:
I will see the goodness of the Lord
in the land of the living.
NIV.14:
Wait for the Lord;
be strong and take heart
and wait for the Lord.

NRSVCE.1:
The LORD is my light and my salvation;
whom shall I fear?
The LORD is the stronghold of my life;
of whom shall I be afraid?
NRSVCE.2:
When evildoers assail me
to devour my flesh —
my adversaries and foes —
they shall stumble and fall.
NRSVCE.3:
Though an army encamp against me,
my heart shall not fear;
though war rise up against me,
yet I will be confident.
NRSVCE.4:
One thing I asked of the LORD,
that will I seek after:
to live in the house of the LORD
all the days of my life,
to behold the beauty of the LORD,
and to inquire in his temple.
NRSVCE.5:
For he will hide me in his shelter
in the day of trouble;
he will conceal me under the cover of his tent;
he will set me high on a rock.
NRSVCE.6:
Now my head is lifted up
above my enemies all around me,
and I will offer in his tent
sacrifices with shouts of joy;
I will sing and make melody to the LORD.
NRSVCE.7:
Hear, O LORD, when I cry aloud,
be gracious to me and answer me!
NRSVCE.8:
“Come,” my heart says, “seek his face!”
Your face, LORD, do I seek.
NRSVCE.9:
Do not hide your face from me.
Do not turn your servant away in anger,
you who have been my help.
Do not cast me off, do not forsake me,
O God of my salvation!
NRSVCE.10:
If my father and mother forsake me,
the LORD will take me up.
NRSVCE.11:
Teach me your way, O LORD,
and lead me on a level path
because of my enemies.
NRSVCE.12:
Do not give me up to the will of my adversaries,
for false witnesses have risen against me,
and they are breathing out violence.
NRSVCE.13:
I believe that I shall see the goodness of the LORD
in the land of the living.
NRSVCE.14:
Wait for the LORD;
be strong, and let your heart take courage;
wait for the LORD!

NET.1: The LORD delivers and vindicates me! I fear no one! The LORD protects my life! I am afraid of no one!
NET.2: When evil men attack me to devour my flesh, when my adversaries and enemies attack me, they stumble and fall.
NET.3: Even when an army is deployed against me, I do not fear. Even when war is imminent, I remain confident.
NET.4: I have asked the LORD for one thing — this is what I desire! I want to live in the LORD’s house all the days of my life, so I can gaze at the splendor of the LORD and contemplate in his temple.
NET.5: He will surely give me shelter in the day of danger; he will hide me in his home; he will place me on an inaccessible rocky summit.
NET.6: Now I will triumph over my enemies who surround me! I will offer sacrifices in his dwelling place and shout for joy! I will sing praises to the LORD!
NET.7: Hear me, O LORD, when I cry out! Have mercy on me and answer me!
NET.8: My heart tells me to pray to you, and I do pray to you, O LORD.
NET.9: Do not reject me! Do not push your servant away in anger! You are my deliverer! Do not forsake or abandon me, O God who vindicates me!
NET.10: Even if my father and mother abandoned me, the LORD would take me in.
NET.11: Teach me how you want me to live; lead me along a level path because of those who wait to ambush me!
NET.12: Do not turn me over to my enemies, for false witnesses who want to destroy me testify against me.
NET.13: Where would I be if I did not believe I would experience the LORD’s favor in the land of the living?
NET.14: Rely on the LORD! Be strong and confident! Rely on the LORD!
